---
title: "Lecture 8: Large-scale, pre-trained language models"
subtitle: >
  {{< var course.code >}}, {{< var course.term >}}
number-sections: false
---

## Lecture overview
### Learning outcomes
- TODO

### Pre-class work
::: {.content-hidden when-profile="student" when-profile="ta"}
#### Instructors
1. TODO

#### Coordinators
1. TODO
:::

::: {.content-hidden when-profile="student"}
#### TAs
1. TODO
:::

#### Learners
1. TODO

### Lecture timeline
::: {.list-table}
- - Topic
  - Time
  - Materials

- - Admin
  - 5 min

- - [Differences from previous lectures](#sec-prev-lectures)
  - 5 min

- - [GPT-3](#sec-gpt-3)
    - Data
    - Parameters
  - 10 min

- - [Pre-trained language model performance](#sec-performance)
  - 20 min

- - [Improving model performance](#sec-improve)
  - 20 min

- - [Wrap-up](#sec-wrap-up)
  - 10 min

- - Admin
  - 5 min
:::

### Post-class work
::: {.content-hidden when-profile="student" when-profile="ta"}
#### Instructors
1. TODO

#### Coordinators
1. TODO
:::

::: {.content-hidden when-profile="student"}
#### TAs
1. TODO
:::

#### Learners
1. TODO

## Differences from previous lectures{#sec-prev-lectures}
- Last lecture, we discussed next-word and next-token prediction, and generated new sentences using a simple next-word predictor.
    - Models that can predict human language patterns like this are called **language models**.
- Suppose we gain access to a neural network that has been trained to perform next-token prediction. We say that a model like this is **pre-trained**: its parameters have already been set, and it already has an idea of language.
- What kinds of tasks could we perform with a model that can already interpret and produce human language?

## GPT-3{#sec-gpt-3}
- In 2020, OpenAI released GPT-3. [TODO: what exactly do we want to say about gpt-3? "largest non-sparse language model" at the time?]
- TODO scale of training data, number of parameters - scaling up self supervised learning
- TODO define **LLM**?
- TODO define **transformer**? instead of looking at only the last word in the sentence, can get a better sense of context we need to draw from all parts of the sentence?

### Data

:::{.callout-tip}
### Activity: Sourcing data
Imagine you're on the GPT-3 development team. You want to train the largest language model yet, and you need a huge amount of text data to do this. Where could you get this data? Are there any possible sources of data you should avoid?
:::

- GPT-3 was trained on a massive amount of text data. This includes most of the Internet, books and news articles, and conversation transcripts.
- However, since no human is manually reviewing all this text, a few problems arise:
    - TODO quality of data - gigo. How bad is this in practice?
    - TODO bias in text making it into the model - hurtful examples and harder to identify examples

### Parameters
- TODO a model with more parameters can learn more complex things - think back to how adding more hidden layers/more neurons per layer in a neural network increased complexity of the relationship that can be learned
- TODO compare scale to GPT-2 (100sB vs 1.5B)
- TODO compare scale of different models including newest GPT, claude

## Pre-trained language model performance{#sec-performance}
- TODO can you just use pre-trained language models to follow instructions? 

:::{.callout-tip}
### Activity: Prompting GPT-3
TODO let them investigate how bad it is to prompt early LM
:::

- TODO what problems do you notice? (repetition etc)

## Improving model performance{#sec-improve}
- TODO how can we improve them? 
    - TODO: fine tuning
        - TODO: catastropic forgetting - lose some generalization here
    - TODO: zero-shot learning/ICL/prompting

:::{.callout-tip}
### Activity: Fine-tuning, zero-shot learning, and ICL
TODO let them see how doing each of these things affects the model
:::

## Wrap-up{#sec-wrap-up}

:::{.callout-tip}
### Activity: Discussion
- TODO
:::
